As with the Hasetsune Cup, we hope you will challenge your limits.

races.name: FUKUI SAKURA MARATHON 2026
races.description: Experience the heart of Fukui like never before at the Fukui Sakura Marathon! Run through breathtaking cherry blossom spots, alongside the sleek Hokuriku Shinkansen, and along a course inspired by the shape of a dinosaur – a truly unique route that lets you feel all of Fukui in one unforgettable race.  Savor the flavors of local cuisine, soak up the warm cheers from spectators, and embrace the vibrant spring atmosphere. Under our inspiring slogan, “Your Passion Blossoms, Sakura Blooms,” let your running spirit flourish and make this spring in Fukui a memory to last a lifetime!

races.name: 28th Ome Takamizu Palatinose Int'l Trail Run
races.description: This race, which began in 1999, is a pioneering domestic trail running competition. Since then, it has become established as a race that signals the start of the domestic trail running season. The race aims for Takamizuyama's "Jofukuin Temple" and Ome's hidden village, "Kurihira Village." The race venue is an 8-minute walk from JR Ome Station. A trail running festival is also planned. races.name: 2026 Aomori Sakura Marathon
races.description: Run Through Aomori’s Scenic and Cultural Highlights!  Start at Nogikawa Park, famous for its cherry blossoms, and finish at Aoi-Umi Park, a beautiful waterfront near Aomori Station, close to attractions like Nebuta House Wa Rasse and A-FACTORY. The course is mostly flat, with a highlight at the Aomori Bay Bridge, featuring a 20-meter elevation change and breathtaking views of Mutsu Bay. Aomori also offers rich cultural experiences – visit historic sites like Sannai-Maruyama and Komakino before or after the race, and enjoy sightseeing in this vibrant city. Experience the beauty and culture of Aomori in a marathon you’ll never forget!

races.description: This marathon event takes place in Kanuma City, where the Kanto Plain meets the Nikko Mountain Range. The course features a mix of urban streets and countryside landscapes, offering runners a scenic view of the Nikko Mountains in the distance. At the finish line, participants can enjoy strawberries, a specialty of Kanuma, known as the "City of Strawberries." Experience the charm of early summer in Kanuma through this marathon.

info.Organizers: Kanuma City, Kanuma City Board of Education, Kanuma City Sports Association, Organized by: Kanuma Satsuki Marathon Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Half marathon: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：180 min. *Cutoff points exist.
Event/Eligibility.10km: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：85 min.*Cutoff points exist.
Event/Eligibility.5km: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：45 min.
Event/Eligibility.3km: Male and Female: Age 12-14, Jogging 15 and above. Time Limit：30 min.
Event/Eligibility.2km: Age 6–8, 9–11, and parents or guardians Age 18 and above. Time Limit：20 min.

info.Participation Fee: Half marathon 4500 JPY, 10km and 5km 4000 JPY, 3km 2000 JPY, 2km 2,500 JPY per group, Age 15-17 2000 JPY, Age 12-14 1500 JPY
info.Check-in Time: Around 7:00AM - 8:00AM
info.Check-in Location: Kanuma City Gotenyama Park Baseball Stadium
info.Participant’s Prize: Original sacoche (medal for the 2km)
info.Award Ceremony: Top 5 in each category (excluding the 3km general men's and women's events and the 2km) *Results will be based on gross time.
info.Capacity: Half marathon 2200 people, 10km 1800 people, 5km 1500 people, 3km 500 people, 2km 1000 pair
info.Transportation Facility: It is a 15-minute walk from Shinkanuma Station on the Tobu Nikko Line, a 20-minute walk from Kanuma Station on the JR Nikko Line, and a free shuttle bus from each station. It is a 5-minute walk from Tenjincho on the Kanto Bus Utsunomiya Line. It is a 20-minute drive from Kanuma IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
info.Other: Parking is available only to residents outside Kanuma City. Spaces are limited, so please use transit. Kanuma City residents cannot use this lot.
info.For Inquiries: Kanuma Satsuki Marathon race Secretariat

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
The 2km race for ages 6 to 8 starts at 9:40AM.

Please register for the 2km race under the name of the participating child age 6 - 11. Additionally, to ensure the safety of participating children, it is mandatory for a guardian or parent to also participate in the race.
During the registration process, you will be asked to confirm whether to purchase a race bib clip. Please check the race bib clip design on the event's official website.
The race guide will be sent by the organizer via email. It is expected to be sent around late April.

During the registration process, you will be asked to confirm Use of Parking Lot. We will be sending parking passes via email to those who wish to use the parking lot. Please print it out and bring it with you.
* This information is current at the time of race registration. Please check the e-mail from the organizer for the final information.
If you are a disabled person and require an escort runner, the participation fee for the escort runner will be waived (up to one person). Please note that the escort runner will not be timed.

races.name: Sendai International Half Marathon 2026
races.description: Run through the forest city of Sendai, colored with fresh greenery. This is one of the best urban half marathon events in Japan, attracting top runners from all over the country as well as citizen runners aiming to finish the race. This year, 10,000 runners are being recruited!

Event/Eligibility.Half marathon (21.0975 km) General divis: Male and Female 18 years and older Time Limit：2 hours 30 minutes, checkpoint exist.

info.Participation Fee: Half marathon 8,000JPY
info.Check-in Time: ［About receiving the race kit］The collection time is scheduled to be between 10:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on May 9 (Sat.)
info.Check-in Location: Koshin Rubber Athlete Park Sendai
info.Check-in Address: 2-11-6 Miyagino, Miyagino-ku, Sendai City, Miyagi Prefecture
info.Start Line: Koshin Rubber Athlete Park Sendai／East side of the road
info.Participant’s Prize: Finishers (those who complete the race within the time limit) will be presented with a finisher's towel.
info.Award Ceremony: Overall and Age Group Awards: Top 8 male and female finishers in the general category (age group awards apply only to the general category). One-Year Increment Awards: Top 10 male and female finishers in each age category, divided by single-year increments.
info.Capacity: Elite division and JAAF registered athlete division: 1,000 runners, General division I and II: 9,000 runners, Wheelchair division: 100 runners*This entry is included in the general division II.
info.Transportation Facility: 5 minutes walk from Miyaginohara Station on the JR Senseki Line
info.For Inquiries: Sendai International Half Marathon Executive Committee

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

In addition, an immediate report of race results of their personal finish time can be confirmed through the Event Results webpage. [Event Results]→ https://runnet.jp/record/top.do

races.name: The 5th Mt. Fuji Sanroku Women's Trail Run
races.description: We support women who are newly challenging trail running in this women-only trail running race. Held at the foot of Mt. Fuji, as the Mt.FUJI100 Friendship Race, participants can enjoy the breathtaking views of Lake Kawaguchiko and Mt. Fuji. It's the perfect event for those making their trail running race debut.

info.Organizers: The General Incorporated Foundation R-bies Sports Foundation

Event/Eligibility.About 13km: Female aged 12 or older Time Limit：3hours

info.Participation Fee: Age 16 or older : 7,000 JPY Ages 12 to 15 : 3,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: Day before the race May 9 2:00PM to 6:00PM, on the race day May 10 7:30AM to 9:30AM
info.Check-in Location: Day before the race, May 9 : Michi-no-Eki Katsuyama,on the race day, May 10 : Koumi Park
info.Check-in Address: Katsuyama, Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi Prefecture
info.Participant’s Prize: Original T-shirts
info.Award Ceremony: 1st to 3rd place, by age group, with special prizes
info.Transportation Facility: About 15minutes by taxi from Fujikyuko Line Kawaguchiko Station.
info.For Inquiries: R-Bees Sports Foundation (Tokyo Office)

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
- The start will be a staggered start.
- This event uses the RUN PASSPORT smart check-in system.
Once your race bib exchange certificate (entry ticket) is ready, we will notify you at the email address you provided during registration.
Please download your entry ticket from the "Entry Ticket Issuance Notice" email, and either save it on your smartphone or print the PDF to bring with you to the event venue.
The email will be sent on April 30th.
＜Notice＞
*If any prohibited actions are discovered, the participant will be disqualified on the spot. When applying for the race, participants are required to agree to the following application regulations.
(1) The entire course is within the designated area of the "National Park" as specified by the Natural Parks Act for environmental conservation.
(2) Pedestrians have priority on the course, even during the competition. Please yield the path when encountering pedestrians.
(3) To protect the vegetation in the Mt. Fuji region, please thoroughly clean both racing and regular shoes. (Strict adherence to prevent the introduction of alien species' seeds, etc.)
(4) Damaging or causing destruction to the course, including forest roads, is strictly prohibited.
(5) Placing objects such as wood and soil on forest roads that obstruct passage is prohibited.
(6) Collection of plants, soil, and similar materials along the forest roads is prohibited.
(7) Parking on forest roads is prohibited.
(8) Absolutely do not leave water and food supplies waste on the course.
(9) Running off the trail is strictly forbidden.
(10) The use of poles or sticks is prohibited.
(11) It is recommended to carry the following items:
・Portable cup (for water)
・Bear bell
races.name_zh: 第五届富士山三六女子越野跑
races.location_zh: 日本山梨县富士河口湖町
races.description_zh: 在这场仅限女性参加的越野跑赛事中，我们将全力支持初次挑战越野跑的女性跑者。赛事作为“富士山100友谊赛”在富士山脚下举行，参赛者可尽享河口湖与富士山令人叹为观止的壮丽景色。对于首次参加越野跑比赛的跑者而言，这是一场再合适不过的赛事。

info_zh.位置: 日本山梨县富士河口湖町
info_zh.主办方: R-bies体育基金会（一般注册基金会）

活动/参赛资格.约13公里: 12岁及以上女性 时限：3小时

info_zh.入住时间: 比赛前一天（5月9日）下午2:00至6:00，比赛当天（5月10日）上午7:30至9:30
info_zh.办理登机手续地点: 比赛前一天，5月9日：胜山道之驿；比赛当天，5月10日：甲斐美公园
info_zh.报到地址: 山梨县南都留郡富士河口湖町胜山
info_zh.起跑线: 河口湖羽子山
info_zh.参与奖: 原创T恤
info_zh.颁奖典礼: 各年龄组的前三名，并设有特别奖
info_zh.交通设施: 从富士急行线河口湖站乘坐出租车约15分钟。
info_zh.咨询: R-Bees体育基金会（东京办事处）

notice_zh: - 请注意，一旦达到最大容量，报名通道将关闭。
- 比赛将采取分批出发的方式。
- 本次活动采用RUN PASSPORT智能签到系统。
一旦您的参赛号码兑换凭证（入场券）准备就绪，我们将通过您在注册时提供的电子邮箱地址通知您。
请从“入场券发放通知”邮件中下载您的入场券，将其保存至智能手机或打印PDF文件，并带至活动现场。
该电子邮件将于4月30日发送。
＜通知＞
*若发现任何违规行为，参赛者将被当场取消参赛资格。报名参赛时，参赛者须同意遵守以下报名规定。
(1) 整个赛道均位于《自然公园法》为环境保护而划定的“国家公园”指定区域内。
(2) 赛道上行人享有优先通行权，即使在比赛期间也是如此。遇到行人时，请礼让。
(3) 为保护富士山地区的植被，请务必彻底清洁跑鞋和普通鞋。（请严格遵守，以防止外来物种的种子等被带入。）
(4) 严禁破坏或损毁赛道，包括林间道路。
(5) 禁止在林道上放置木材、泥土等物品，阻碍通行。
(6) 禁止在林道沿线采集植物、土壤及类似材料。
(7) 禁止在林道上停车。
(8) 切勿将水和食物残渣遗留在赛道上。
(9) 严禁在小径外奔跑。
(10) 禁止使用长杆或棍棒。
(11) 建议携带以下物品：
・便携杯（装水用）
・防熊铃

races.name: Karuizawa Half Marathon 2026
races.description: This is a fantastic opportunity to run through Karuizawa, one of Japan's premier resort destinations, during the refreshing season of lush greenery! With the Shinkansen station just an 8-minute walk away, access is incredibly convenient. After the race, enjoy shopping at the Karuizawa Prince Shopping Plaza, strolling through the charming streets, or savoring local cuisineeverything about Karuizawa promises a delightful experience!

info.Organizers: Karuizawa town, Karuizawa Tourisｍ Association, Wakaba Matsuri committee, The Shinano Mainichi Shimbun

Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon (21.0975km): Male and Female Age 19 or older, grouped by 19–29, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60s, and 70 and above. Time Limit：2 hours 50 minutes, there are 4 checkpoints.
Event/Eligibility.Family Pair (2.1km): Family (parents aged 20 or older and up to two children age 6-12.※Not necessarily to be parent and child Time Limit：20 minutes

info.Finish Line: Karuizawa Prince Hotel Ski Resort Parking Lot

info.Award Ceremony: Half Marathon Overall (Men/Women) & Family Pair: 6th Place, Half Marathon (Men/Women by Age Group): 3rd Place in Each Division
info.Capacity: Half Marathon (21.0975km): 5,500 people, Family Pair (2.1km): 250 pairs (500 people)

races.description_zh: 这是一个绝佳的机会，让您在绿意盎然的清新时节，畅跑于日本顶级度假胜地之一——轻井泽！新干线车站仅需步行8分钟即可到达，交通极为便利。赛后，您可以在轻井泽王子购物广场尽情购物，漫步于迷人的街道，或品尝当地美食——轻井泽的每一处都将为您带来愉悦的体验！

info_zh.参与奖: T恤 *半程马拉松完赛者将获赠一条完赛毛巾；以家庭为单位参赛且均完成比赛的6至12岁儿童将获赠一枚完赛奖牌。

races.description_zh: 来一场畅快的跑步之旅吧！穿越日本樱桃产量第一的城市——山形县东根市，这里也是“佐藤锦”樱桃的发源地！这是一条樱桃如红宝石般璀璨夺目的马拉松赛道。起终点设在日本陆上自卫队基地内，您将有机会在自卫队基地内奔跑。我们将为所有参赛者提供“GI东根佐藤锦”樱桃！

info_zh.主办方: 山形县东根市

活动/参赛资格.10公里: 性别：16-18岁、19-29岁、30多岁、40多岁、50多岁、60岁以上 时间限制：总计1小时35分钟 → 检查点设在5.5公里处（55分钟）和7.6公里处（1小时15分钟）。
活动/参赛资格.5公里: 性别：16-18岁、19-29岁、30多岁、40多岁、50多岁、60岁以上 时间限制：总计50分钟 → 2.8公里处设检查点（30分钟）。
活动/参赛资格.3 公里: 男女组：10-12岁、13-15岁；亲子组（2人或3人一队）：监护人（18岁及以上）+ 儿童（7-9岁）；时间限制：总计30分钟 → 无检查点。

info_zh.入住时间: 【6月7日】上午6:30——赛事当天办理报到手续时，将发放包含号码牌在内的参赛包。
info_zh.办理登机手续地点: 日本陆上自卫队神町营地
info_zh.报到地址: 山形县东根市神町南3-1-1
info_zh.起跑线: 日本陆上自卫队神町营地（东根市神町南3-1-1）
info_zh.终点线: 日本陆上自卫队神町营地
info_zh.参与奖: GI东根樱桃“佐藤锦”、使用山形县大米制作的饭团、水野锦标赛纪念T恤、冷冻水果
info_zh.颁奖典礼: 每个组别的前三名将获得奖项。半程马拉松的男女总冠军也将获得奖品。
info_zh.交通设施: 提供从JR樱桃东根站和樱桃东根温泉出发的免费接驳巴士：距东北中央高速公路东根IC约10分钟车程，距山形高速公路山形北IC约30分钟车程。

races.name: Nordisk Mountain Trail The 17th Fuji Oshino Kogen Trail Race
races.description: The Mt. FUJI100 Friendship Race Series 2026 will be held. With ample water stations and course guidance, this event is enjoyable for everyone from beginners to advanced runners. Participants will receive a T-shirt, pork miso soup or soba noodles after finishing, and a towel. Starting with the 2026 event, a new Family Run category will be introduced.

Event/Eligibility.Approx.21.6km: Male ＆Female, Ages 15 or older Time Limit：Final wave: 6 hours, with checkpoints
Event/Eligibility.Approx.13.2km: Male ＆Female, Ages 13 or older Time Limit：Final wave: 5 hours, with checkpoints

races.name: The 24th RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING
races.description: The 24th RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING event offers participants a unique opportunity to enjoy an extraordinary course that circles the island. Runners will be treated to breathtaking views, including the refreshing coastal scenery with the scent of the sea, the majestic Mt. Rishiri—one of Japan’s famed 100 mountains—and the picturesque sight of Hokkaido across the water.

info.Organizers: RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Approx. 53.67 km: Male/Female: 13 years old and above Time Limit：10 hours with checkpoints.

info.Participation Fee: Adults: 10,000 JPY, Ages 13-18 years old: 2,000 JPY

info.Transportation Facility: Three ferry services are available between Wakkanai and Rishiri (15 minutes on foot from Oshidomari Port). One flight each between New Chitose and Rishiri (available from June 1) and between Okadama and Rishiri (10 minutes by bus from Rishiri Airport).
info.For Inquiries: RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING Executive Committee

races.description: This is a unique rural trail race that features a collection of art installations from the Echigo-Tsumari Art Triennale (ETAT), beautiful terraced rice fields certified as "Connecting Terraced Heritage" by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries, and revitalized ancient paths. At the aid stations, you can enjoy local cuisine cultivated in snowy regions! After overcoming the harsh winter, experience the deep green of spring in Echigo Matsudai and the pristine landscapes of Japan. Please enjoy the race!

races.description_zh: 这是一场独具特色的乡村越野跑赛事，沿途不仅能欣赏到越后妻有艺术三年展（ETAT）的艺术装置群，还有被日本农林水产省认证为“梯田遗产”的美丽梯田，以及经过修复的古道。在补给站，您还可以品尝到雪国孕育的当地美食！ 在战胜严冬之后，请尽情体验越后松代的春日浓绿与日本纯净的自然风光。祝您比赛愉快！

=====Eligibility======

Those who have participated in and completed a race recognized by the International Trail Running Association (ITRA), and have earned the prescribed points of "10 points or more in up to 3 races" or "6 points or more in 1 race".
①Points must have been earned from September 26, 2021 to September 25, 2025.
However, at least one race must be from September 26, 2024 to September 25, 2025.

②Completed the "DEEP JAPAN ULTRA 100 Official Test Run Camp" on June 7-8, 2025 or November 1-2, 2025. within the time allotted will be allowed to participate in the race without the required ITRA points.
Those who meet any of the following conditions.
①Those who have completed a trail race of 30km or longer between January 1, 2020 and September 25, 2025.
②Those who participated in the 'DEEP JAPAN ULTRA 100 Official Test Run Camp' on the first day (Saturday, June 7, 2025, or November 1) and completed it within the time limit.
- In the event of inaccurate, non-compliant or misleading statements regarding stipulated points, the Organizer will cancel the registration without refunding the entry fee.
When you apply, you will be asked to provide a WhatsApp number where you can be contacted in Japan. Please have it ready.
※ Note that for WhatsApp only voice calls are accepted as the contact method.

Since this event started in January 2021 with the hope of revitalizing both the sports community and the local economy of Minamiaso Village, it has been held twice a year in Summer and Winter. Come and experience the breathtaking natural beauty of Minamiaso in summer. We look forward to welcoming you to the race!

notice_zh: - 若活动名额已满，报名可能会在截止日期前提前截止。

- 请在比赛当天，至少在您的起跑时间前30分钟到咨询处领取号码布。

参赛者必须在2024年2月20日至报名之时期间，至少完成过一次30公里或更长的越野跑赛事。

- 必填申请信息
申请时，请为两位团队成员提供以下信息：
姓名｜性别｜出生日期｜手机号码｜参赛原因｜T恤购买需求（可选）｜住宿安排（比赛前一天及比赛当天）｜紧急联系人信息（姓名、关系、电话号码）

支付方式：现金、PayPay 或信用卡（仅限活动当天）

races.name: CENTRAL ALPS SKYLINE JAPAN 2026
races.description: The 7th CENTRAL ALPS SKYLINE JAPAN will take place at the foothills of the Central Alps, one of Japan’s major mountain ranges. With its dramatic landscapes and rugged terrain, the Central Alps provide an ideal setting for challengers seeking an authentic mountain challenge.

races.name_zh: 日本中央阿尔卑斯天际线 2026
races.location_zh: 日本长野县小金井市/伊那市/宫田村
races.description_zh: 第七届“中央阿尔卑斯天际线日本赛”将在日本主要山脉之一——中央阿尔卑斯山脉的山麓举行。中央阿尔卑斯山脉拥有壮丽的景观和崎岖的地形，为寻求真正山地挑战的参赛者提供了理想的舞台。

notice_zh: - 请注意，一旦达到最大容量，报名通道将关闭。

- 本次活动为计时赛，排名将根据用时最短者确定。- 比赛指南将于报名截止后约一周通过电子邮件发送。
【赛道距离】
CASJ 90k：86.7公里，爬升5,285米，下降5,266米
-能够轻松完成赛道距离超过50公里且累计爬升超过3000米的越野跑，并具备在海拔2000米以上地区登山经验的人士
NIPPON TRGP CENTRAL ALPS 38k：37.5公里，爬升2,571米，下降2,597米
- 能够轻松完成赛道距离超过30公里且累计爬升超过2000米的越野跑，并具备在海拔2000米以上地区登山经验的人士
HIGHLAND 20k：21.0公里，爬升1,245米，下降1,242米
- 能够轻松跑完20公里以上越野路线的人
KOMAKAPPA 10公里赛：10.2公里，爬升628米，下降636米
- 想要尝试越野跑的人，以及对自己体能有信心的人
MIYASAN 4k：4.0公里，爬升86米，下降85米
- 既能享受维护良好的步行道，又能迎接挑战的人士，以及想要体验高原风光的家庭 *学龄前儿童必须由监护人陪同参加
*“家庭挑战”的申请应以父母/监护人（19岁以上）的名义提交。

races.name: The 18th Tsumagoi Kogen Cabbage Marathon
races.description: To celebrate Japan’s well-known “Devoted Husbands” tradition, this race features a special division for husband-and-wife pairs. Known as one of Japan’s toughest road races, the intense out-and-back course of the Tsumagoi Highland Cabbage Marathon is bound to excite any marathon enthusiast! It’s more than just a race—it’s a fun-filled celebration of all things cabbage!

info.Organizers: Tsumagoi Kogen Cabbage Marathon Executive Committee, Tsumagoi Village

Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon: Male/Female: 16 years old and above Time Limit：Total of 3 hours (Until 12:00 PM)
Event/Eligibility.10 km: Male/Female: 16+, Couple pairs (Married couples, Dating couples and Engaged couples can enter) Time Limit：Until 12:00 PM
Event/Eligibility.5.4 km: Male/Female: 16 years old and above, 13-15 years old Time Limit：Until 12:00 PM
Event/Eligibility.2 km: Male/Female: 7-12 years old Time Limit：Until 12:00 PM
Event/Eligibility.2 km【Parent & Child Division】: Parent & Child pairs: One parent and one child (Children must be 0-12 years old) Time Limit：Until 12:00 PM

info.Participation Fee: 【General】5,500 JPY,【Parent & Child pair】7,000 JPY,【7-15 years old】3,500 JPY,【Couple pair】11,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Day before race day), 5:00 AM - 8:00 AM (Race day)
info.Check-in Location: Tokai University Tsumagoi Kogen Training Center Multipurpose Grounds

info.Award Ceremony: Certificates and prizes (medals for the top three) are awarded to the top six finishers. Categories→Men: High school & older (≤29, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60+), junior high, elementary (Grades 1–3 / 4–6). Women: High school & older (≤34, 35–49, 50+), junior high, elementary (Grades 1–3 / 4–6) No parent-child or couples divisions. Grades based on the JP school system.
info.Transportation Facility: Approximately 30 minutes by car from JR Agatsuma Line Manza-Kazawaguchi Station
info.Other: We kindly request that you carpool whenever possible in order to participate.
info.For Inquiries: Tsumagoi Kogen Cabbage Marathon Executive Committee

notice: 【Parent & Child Pair Division】
This division is open to teams consisting of one parent or guardian and one child aged 0–12.
Please register under the name of the parent or guardian who will run together with the child.
【Couple Pair Division】
Open to married couples, engaged couples, or couples in a relationship.
Participants in this division are required to stay at a designated accommodation on the night before the race.
Please contact the Tsumagoi Village Tourism Association to book eligible accommodations.
Email: info@tsumagoi-kankou.jp
【Minors】
Participants under the age of 19 must obtain parental consent prior to registration.
【Wheelchair Participation】
Wheelchair participation is not permitted in this event.
【Participants with Disabilities】
Participants who require assistance may be accompanied by one escort at no additional charge.
Please note that guide dogs are not permitted.
Escorts must be arranged by the participant in advance.
For further information, please contact the event office.
【Tourism Inquiries】
For tourism-related inquiries, please contact the Tsumagoi Village Tourism Association.
Email: info@tsumagoi-kankou.jp
races.name_zh: 第18届津轻高原卷心菜马拉松
races.description_zh: 为庆祝日本著名的“忠夫”传统，本届赛事特设夫妻组别。作为日本最艰苦的公路赛之一，嬰驹高原卷心菜马拉松那条充满挑战的往返赛道，定会让每位马拉松爱好者热血沸腾！这不仅仅是一场赛事——更是一场充满乐趣的卷心菜主题庆典！

info_zh.主办方: 嬰子高原高麗菜馬拉松執行委員會，嬰子村

活动/参赛资格.半程马拉松: 性别：16岁及以上 时限：共计3小时（截至中午12:00）
活动/参赛资格.10公里: 性别：16岁以上，情侣组（已婚夫妇、正在约会的伴侣及订婚情侣均可报名） 截止时间：中午12:00
活动/参赛资格.5.4 公里: 性别：16岁及以上，13-15岁 截止时间：中午12:00
活动/参赛资格.2公里: 性别：7-12岁 截止时间：中午12:00

info_zh.颁奖典礼: 前六名选手将获得证书和奖品（前三名将获得奖牌）。组别→男子：高中及以上（≤29岁、30岁组、40岁组、50岁组、60岁及以上组）、初中、小学（1–3年级 / 4–6年级）。 女子组：高中及以上（≤34岁、35–49岁、50岁以上）、初中、小学（1–3年级 / 4–6年级）。不设亲子组或情侣组。年级划分依据横滨市（JP）学制。

notice_zh: 【亲子组】
本组别面向由一名家长或监护人及一名0至12岁儿童组成的队伍。
请以将与孩子一同参赛的家长或监护人的名义进行注册。

【轮椅参与】
本赛事不允许轮椅选手参赛。
【残障人士】
需要协助的参与者可由一名陪同人员陪同，无需额外付费。
请注意，导盲犬不得入内。
陪同人员须由参与者提前安排。
如需了解更多信息，请联系活动办公室。
【旅游咨询】
如需咨询旅游相关事宜，请联系津轻村旅游协会。
电子邮件：info@tsumagoi-kankou.jp

A special category for first-time runners will also be available, making it accessible for all. We look forward to welcoming you to Kita-Hiroshima this early summer!

races.name: The 79th Fuji Mountain Race
races.description: Take on Japan's toughest mountain race, combining urban road, cross-country, and mountain elements. Challenge yourself on Mt. Fuji, Japan's highest peak!

info.Organizers: Fujiyoshida City

Event/Eligibility.Approx. 15 km (5th Station Course): Male/Female: 18+ Time Limit：Total of 3 hrs & 30 mins with a checkpoint at Uma-gaeshi (11.4 km)→ 2 hrs

In addition, awards will be given to the 6th place overall male and female finishers in the 21 km Summit Course and the 15 km 5th Station Course, as well as to the top 3 finishers in each age group in the 21 km Summit Course. The Ironman Award will be presented to the four oldest male and four olde

races.name: Daisetsuzan Trail Journey 2026
races.description: As the most attractive highlights of the course, the route connecting Tengu-dake from Hirayama, located on the northern side of the Daisetsuzan mountain range is offering the beautiful 360 degrees panoramic view.  You can also see numerous alpine plants, including Komakusa.  A majestic view of the main peaks of Daisetsuzan, including Mt. Kurodake and Mt. Asahidake and endlessly stretching vast farmland－This is a fantastic course where you can enjoy breathtaking views and this is what Hokkaido scenery is all about!

Completion of other trail running events is not required, but completion of at least one trail running race of 30km or longer, or one road race of 40km or longer, is desirable.
Completion of other trail running events is not required, but completion of at least one trail running race of 20km or longer, or one road race of 30km or longer, is desirable.

races.description_zh: 作为本路线最引人入胜的亮点，从位于大雪山脉北侧的平山通往天狗岳的路线，可欣赏到壮丽的360度全景。  沿途还能观赏到包括小蓟在内的众多高山植物。黑岳、旭岳等大雪山主峰的雄伟景色，以及一望无际的广阔农田——这是一条能让您尽享令人屏息美景的绝佳路线，正是北海道风光的精髓所在！

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
- This event is an International Trail Running Association (ITRA) Points Race. The Long Course is eligible for 1 ITRA point.

- The organizer reserves the right to change or shorten the course, or to cancel the race, at their discretion due to unforeseen circumstances such as earthquakes, severe weather, epidemics, incidents, or reports of bear activity.

In the event that the race is canceled due to reasons beyond the organizer’s control—including earthquakes, heavy rain, epidemics, or bear activity—entry fees will not be refunded, including travel and accommodation expenses.
＜Mandatory Equipment>
In addition to the mandatory equipment listed below, participants are responsible for bringing any additional equipment they deem necessary.
- Water (Long Course: at least 1 liter / Short Course: at least 500 ml) and nutrition
- Mobile phone (Must be fully charged, registered with the race organizer’s contact number, and set to display the caller ID)
- To reduce the risk of getting lost, participants are strongly encouraged to carry a smartphone capable of running navigation apps such as YAMAP, along with a spare battery.
- The race organizer’s contact number is printed on the number card.
- Personal cup or similar item (Disposable paper cups will not be provided in order to reduce resource consumption.)
- Bear bell
- To protect vegetation, the use of trekking poles is strictly prohibited on all courses.
- In addition, wearing earphones during the race is prohibited for safety reasons.

notice_zh: - 请注意，一旦达到最大容量，报名通道将关闭。
- 本赛事为国际越野跑协会（ITRA）积分赛。长距离组可获得1个ITRA积分。
- 主办方保留因地震、恶劣天气、疫情、突发事件或熊出没报告等不可预见的情况，自行决定更改或缩短赛程，或取消比赛的权利。
若因主办方无法控制的原因（包括地震、暴雨、疫情或熊出没等）导致比赛取消，报名费将不予退还，包括交通及住宿费用。
＜必备装备＞
除以下列出的必备装备外，参与者还需自行携带其认为必要的其他装备。
- 饮用水（长距离赛：至少1升 / 短距离赛：至少500毫升）及补给
- 手机（必须充满电，已存入赛事组委会的联系电话，并设置为显示来电显示）
- 为降低迷路的风险，强烈建议参与者随身携带可运行YAMAP等导航应用的智能手机，并备好备用电池。
- 赛事组织方的联系电话印在号码牌上。
- 自带杯子或其他类似容器（为减少资源消耗，现场不提供一次性纸杯。）
- 驱熊铃
- 为保护植被，所有赛道上严禁使用登山杖。
- 此外，出于安全考虑，比赛期间禁止佩戴耳机。

races.name: 43rd Omachi Alps Marathon
races.description: Shinano Omachi offers breathtaking views of the Northern Alps ridgeline, where the crisp autumn breeze and clear blue skies set the stage for an unforgettable journey. As you run, the scenery transforms from majestic mountain foothills to charming countryside and serene lakesides—a stunning course that’s sure to captivate you. Since 1983, this historic event has welcomed runners under the theme "Run across the Roof of Japan." It’s also the birthplace of Nagano Prefecture's first-ever full marathon open to citizen runners. Don’t miss out on this iconic race!

races.name_zh: 第43届大町阿尔卑斯马拉松
races.description_zh: 信浓大町拥有北阿尔卑斯山脉山脊线令人屏息的壮丽景色，清冽的秋风与湛蓝的天空共同为这场难忘的旅程营造出绝佳的氛围。 奔跑途中，景色从雄伟的山麓逐渐转变为迷人的田园风光和宁静的湖畔——这条令人惊叹的赛道定会令您沉醉其中。自1983年以来，这项历史悠久的赛事一直以“奔跑在日本屋脊”为主题，欢迎跑者们前来参与。这里也是长野县首场面向普通跑者的全程马拉松的发源地。千万不要错过这场标志性的赛事！

Along the way, you’ll be cheered on by enthusiastic supporters and treated to stunning sights, including the breathtaking Toyama Bay—proudly a part of the "World's Most Beautiful Bays Club"—the majestic Tateyama Mountain Range, and the iconic Takaoka Daibutsu. Join us for this unforgettable 11th Toyama Marathon and feel the incredible sense of accomplishment as you cross the finish line!

*注册时将核验身份证件，以确认注册人的身份。
* 如果参会者本人无法亲自前来办理注册手续，可由他人代为办理。在此情况下，代办人必须出示：①参会者的入场券（可为手机上的PDF文件或打印件）、②参会者的授权书、③参会者的身份证复印件，以及④代办人本人的身份证。
- 申报成绩为3小时29分59秒或更短的参赛者，须通过Foam平台向赛事组委会提交2023年11月5日之后参加的国内或国际全程马拉松赛事的完赛证书复印件。
请在此上传时间记录。
*不接受线上马拉松。
*总时间或净时间均可。
*若未在6月30日（周二）前提交，参赛者将从后排出发。
*申报的时间必须与比赛证书上记录的时间一致。若两者不一致，则以证书上记录的时间为准。
*已申报2023年、2024年及2025年富山马拉松成绩的参赛者无需提交成绩记录。
